benchmark_name: McKinsey productivity lift from social/collaboration tools
context: McKinsey Global Institute: 'The Social Economy' report; estimated benefit from full implementation of social technologies in workplace
reliability_notes: Primary report (McKinsey); widely cited; published 2012 so pre-dates Slack but establishes market rationale

benchmark_name: McKinsey addressable economic value from collaboration tools
context: McKinsey Global Institute: 'The Social Economy'; projected value across US industries from social technology adoption
reliability_notes: Same primary report; macro-level projection; used as TAM narrative anchor

context: MarketsandMarkets / analyst consortium aggregates from 2015–2016 forward-looking reports; covers unified communications, collaboration, messaging
reliability_notes: Secondary aggregation; range reflects variation across report definitions; treat as directional

context: Subset of broader collaboration market; 2016-era analyst reports; projected growth to ~$16.6B by 2025 at ~9-10% CAGR
reliability_notes: Secondary aggregation from GlobeNewswire, Mordor Intelligence; treat as directional

context: Forward-looking from 2016 analyst reports; team collaboration software segment
reliability_notes: Secondary; range across multiple analyst sources

benchmark_name: Knowledge worker time spent on email
metric: Share of workweek devoted to managing email
context: Research cited in Forbes 2014 ('Email Overload Is Costing You Billions'); attributed to McKinsey and related studies
reliability_notes: Secondary synthesis (Forbes summarizing McKinsey + other studies); widely cited figure

benchmark_name: Email overload cost to large organizations
context: Studies referenced in 2014 estimate millions per year in lost productivity for large enterprises due to email overload
reliability_notes: Imprecise — no single definitive figure; directional anchor only

benchmark_name: SaaS net dollar retention — median (IPO cohort)
metric: Median NDR at time of SaaS IPO
context: Sammy Abdullah / Blossom Street Ventures analysis of 2017–2021 SaaS IPOs; median NDR benchmark
reliability_notes: Retrospective benchmark applied back to 2016–2017 SaaS norms; not a 2014–2017 primary report

benchmark_name: SaaS net dollar retention — top performers (IPO cohort)
context: Same cohort; top-quintile threshold from Sammy Abdullah / Blossom Street analysis
Slack S-1 (Apr 2019); places Slack well above median (114%) and top-quintile (120%+) thresholds; establishes expansion economics
reliability_notes: From S-1 filing; apply cautiously to 2014–2017 window — exact fiscal year within S-1 not confirmed in user submission
